title: Raddia calypsoides
intro: Raddia calypsoides, also known as Calabar berry or Monkey apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. The health benefits of Raddia calypsoides include its potential antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including malaria and fever. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Raddia calypsoides include tea infusions and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as to repel insects and parasites.
taxonomy: Raddia calypsoides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. It is native to South America and belongs to the genus Raddia. The genus Raddia is part of the order Urticales, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their nettle-like leaves. The classification of Raddia calypsoides also places it within the clade Asterids, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This classification highlights the unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships of this plant species.
